project.name: stne-log-parser
project.description: log parsing library for STNE ingame logs

nodes.name: avatar-job.js
nodes.summary: Enum of avatar job roles used to classify and tag avatar-related log lines in the parser.

nodes.name: AvatarJob
nodes.summary: Enumify-based class enumerating the possible avatar professions (geologist, weapons officer, doctor, etc.) used to tag avatar action lines.

nodes.name: drone-pilot-drone-type.js
nodes.summary: Enum of drone pilot drone categories (attack, decoy) used to distinguish drone-type events in parsed log lines.

nodes.name: DronePilotDroneType
nodes.summary: Enumify class enumerating the two drone categories operated by the drone pilot avatar: attack drone and decoy drone.

nodes.name: armor-absorption-type.js
nodes.summary: Parser for armor-absorption log lines: matches ship name and absorption points, then credits the ship's armor statistics.

nodes.name: ArmorAbsorptionType
nodes.summary: Parses German and English log lines describing armor of a ship absorbing attack damage and registers the absorbed points in ship statistics.

nodes.name: avatar-attack-drone-crit-type.js
nodes.summary: Parser for avatar attack-drone critical hits: records the critical strike as a successful avatar action in statistics.

nodes.name: AvatarAttackDroneCritType
nodes.summary: Parses avatar attack-drone critical-hit log lines, extracting the avatar, ship, and drone critical-strike context into the result and avatar statistics.

nodes.name: avatar-attack-drone-destruction-type.js
nodes.summary: Parser for avatar attack-drone destruction events: records drone loss in statistics.

nodes.name: AvatarAttackDroneDestructionType
nodes.summary: Parses log lines reporting the destruction of an avatar's attack drone, capturing the avatar and the affected ship into the result.

nodes.name: avatar-attack-drone-launch-type.js
nodes.summary: Parser for avatar attack-drone launch events: registers the launch as an avatar action in statistics.

nodes.name: AvatarAttackDroneLaunchType
nodes.summary: Parses log lines announcing the launch of an avatar's attack drone, identifying the launching avatar and the associated ship.

nodes.name: avatar-boarding-eps-damage-type.js
nodes.summary: Parser for avatar boarding actions that produce energy damage to ship and countermeasures: records the action and its success in statistics.

nodes.name: AvatarBoardingEpsDamageType
nodes.summary: Parses boarding-action log lines that deal direct and countermeasure energy damage, capturing avatar, both ships, and the parsed damage values.

nodes.name: avatar-boarding-intercepted-type.js
nodes.summary: Parser for intercepted boarding actions: credits the boarding avatar with an action and the intercepting avatar with a successful counter-action.

nodes.name: AvatarBoardingInterceptedType
nodes.summary: Parses boarding actions intercepted by another avatar, identifying the boarding avatar, the intercepting avatar, and the contested ship.

nodes.name: avatar-boarding-retreat-type.js
nodes.summary: Parser for avatar boarding retreats: registers the retreat as an avatar action in statistics.

nodes.name: AvatarBoardingRetreatType
nodes.summary: Parses avatar boarding-retreat log lines, identifying the avatar pulling back and the ship it leaves.

nodes.name: avatar-boarding-weapon-sabotage-type.js
nodes.summary: Parser for avatar weapon-sabotage boarding actions: records the action and its success in avatar statistics.

nodes.name: AvatarBoardingWeaponSabotageType
nodes.summary: Parses boarding actions that sabotage the weapons of a target ship, capturing the boarding avatar, the target ship, and the affected weapon system.

nodes.name: avatar-damage-reduction-failure-type.js
nodes.summary: Parser for failed avatar damage-reduction attempts: registers the failed action in avatar statistics.

nodes.name: AvatarDamageReductionFailureType
nodes.summary: Parses log lines where an avatar's damage-reduction attempt fails, capturing the avatar, source ship, target colony, and attacker.

nodes.name: avatar-damage-reduction-type.js
nodes.summary: Parser for avatar damage-reduction successes: credits the avatar with a successful damage-reduction action in statistics.

nodes.name: AvatarDamageReductionType
nodes.summary: Parses successful avatar damage-reduction log lines, capturing the avatar, source ship, target colony, attacker, and the damage amount absorbed.

nodes.name: avatar-decoy-drone-failure-type.js
nodes.summary: Parser for failed decoy-drone attempts: records the failed action in avatar statistics.

nodes.name: AvatarDecoyDroneFailureType
nodes.summary: Parses log lines where a decoy drone attempt fails, identifying the avatar, the ship being protected, the colony, and the attacker.

nodes.name: avatar-decoy-drone-success-type.js
nodes.summary: Parser for successful decoy-drone deployments: credits the avatar with a successful decoy-drone action in statistics.

nodes.name: AvatarDecoyDroneSuccessType
nodes.summary: Parses successful decoy-drone log lines, identifying the avatar, the protected ship, the colony, and the attacker.

nodes.name: avatar-emergency-shield-activation-failure-type.js
nodes.summary: Parser for failed avatar emergency-shield activations: records the failed action in avatar statistics.

nodes.name: AvatarEmergencyShieldActivationFailureType
nodes.summary: Parses log lines where an avatar's emergency shield activation fails, identifying the avatar and the affected ship.

nodes.name: avatar-emergency-shield-activation-type.js
nodes.summary: Parser for successful avatar emergency-shield activations: credits the avatar with a successful defensive action in statistics.

nodes.name: AvatarEmergencyShieldActivationType
nodes.summary: Parses successful avatar emergency-shield-activation log lines, identifying the avatar and the protected ship.

nodes.name: avatar-energy-recovery-type.js
nodes.summary: Parser for avatar energy-recovery events: registers the recovery as a successful avatar action in statistics.

nodes.name: AvatarEnergyRecoveryType
nodes.summary: Parses avatar energy-recovery log lines, identifying the avatar, ship, and the amount of main energy recovered.

nodes.name: avatar-out-of-drones-type.js
nodes.summary: Parser for 'out of drones' events: maps the German drone-type label to the DronePilotDroneType enum and records the action in statistics.

nodes.name: AvatarOutOfDronesType
nodes.summary: Parses log lines where an avatar is out of attack or decoy drones, identifying the avatar, ship, opponent (ship or colony), and drone type via the DronePilotDroneType enum.

nodes.name: avatar-pilot-maneuver-cooldown-type.js
nodes.summary: Parser for pilot-maneuver cooldown events: registers the cooldown as a non-successful avatar action in statistics.

nodes.name: AvatarPilotManeuverCooldownType
nodes.summary: Parses log lines where a pilot avatar is in maneuver cooldown, identifying the avatar and the ship.

nodes.name: avatar-pilot-maneuver-failure-type.js
nodes.summary: Parser for failed pilot maneuvers: registers the failed action in avatar statistics.

nodes.name: AvatarPilotManeuverFailureType
nodes.summary: Parses log lines where a pilot maneuver fails, identifying the avatar and the ship.

nodes.name: avatar-pilot-maneuver-success-type.js
nodes.summary: Parser for successful pilot maneuvers: credits the avatar with a successful maneuver action in statistics.

nodes.name: AvatarPilotManeuverSuccessType
nodes.summary: Parses successful pilot-maneuver log lines, identifying the avatar, the source ship, and the target ship.

nodes.name: avatar-pilot-maneuver-type.js
nodes.summary: Parser for generic avatar pilot maneuvers: registers the maneuver as an avatar action in statistics.

nodes.name: AvatarPilotManeuverType
nodes.summary: Parses generic pilot-maneuver log lines, identifying the avatar and the ship, used as the base type for cooldown, success, and failure variants.

nodes.name: avatar-weak-point-miss-type.js
nodes.summary: Parser for avatar weak-point misses: records the missed attack as an avatar action in statistics.

nodes.name: AvatarWeakPointMissType
nodes.summary: Parses log lines where an avatar misses a weak-point attack, identifying the avatar and the ship.

nodes.name: avatar-weapon-damage-increase-type.js
nodes.summary: Parser for avatar weapon-damage-increase events: credits the avatar with a successful damage-buff action in statistics.

nodes.name: AvatarWeaponDamageIncreaseType
nodes.summary: Parses log lines where an avatar increases weapon damage, identifying the avatar, both ships, both colonies, and the damage bonus.

nodes.name: declare-war-type.js
nodes.summary: Parser for war-declaration events: registers the declaring player in the player-character statistics index.

nodes.name: DeclareWarType
nodes.summary: Parses German war-declaration log lines, extracting the declaring player's name and ID and registering the player in player-character statistics.

nodes.name: energy-damage-type.js
nodes.summary: Parser for main-energy damage events: handles both normal damage and disabled-ship variants, then registers the ship in statistics.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses optional non-capturing regex branches and German decimal-comma normalization via String.prototype.replace to parse localized numbers.

nodes.name: EnergyDamageType
nodes.summary: Parses German and English main-energy damage log lines, extracting the ship, damage amount, prior and remaining energy levels, and a 'ship disabled' flag from optional regex branches.

nodes.name: environment-cerulean-type.js
nodes.summary: Parser for cerulean-environment energy-loss events: registers the affected ship in statistics.

nodes.name: EnvironmentCeruleanType
nodes.summary: Parses cerulean-environment log lines describing ship energy loss in a cerulean nebula or radiation zone.

nodes.name: hull-damage-type.js
nodes.summary: Parser for hull-damage events: registers the affected ship in statistics and tracks damage, overkill, and remaining integrity.

nodes.name: HullDamageType
nodes.summary: Parses German and English hull-damage log lines, extracting the ship, damage amount, overkill damage, and remaining hull strength.

nodes.name: item-weapon-overload-type.js
nodes.summary: Line-type parser for item-weapon-overload log lines. Extends GenericType, defines a German regex that captures a target ship and damage increase, and produces an ItemWeaponOverloadResult.

nodes.name: ItemWeaponOverloadType
nodes.summary: Static-method line-type parser that matches the German 'Waffenüberladung' overload line, builds an ItemWeaponOverloadResult (target ship + damage increase), registers the target in statistics, and emits battle/item tags.

nodes.name: armor-absorption-result.js
nodes.summary: Data class for armor absorption parse results, holding the affected ship and the damage points absorbed by armor.

nodes.name: ArmorAbsorptionResult
nodes.summary: Typed DTO with JSDoc-annotated fields: ship (ShipNameAndNccResult) and armorAbsorption (number).

nodes.name: avatar-attack-drone-crit-result.js
nodes.summary: Data class for a critical attack-drone hit by an avatar, holding the avatar and the target ship.

nodes.name: AvatarAttackDroneCritResult
nodes.summary: Typed DTO with fields avatar (AvatarResult) and ship (ShipNameAndNccResult) describing a crit drone attack.

nodes.name: avatar-attack-drone-destruction-result.js
nodes.summary: Data class for an avatar destroying an attack drone, holding the avatar and the target ship.

nodes.name: AvatarAttackDroneDestructionResult
nodes.summary: Typed DTO with fields avatar (AvatarResult) and ship (ShipNameAndNccResult) describing a destroyed attack drone.

nodes.name: avatar-attack-drone-launch-result.js
nodes.summary: Data class for an avatar launching an attack drone, holding the avatar and the launching ship.

nodes.name: AvatarAttackDroneLaunchResult
nodes.summary: Typed DTO with fields avatar (AvatarResult) and ship (ShipNameAndNccResult) describing a drone launch.

nodes.name: avatar-boarding-eps-damage-result.js
nodes.summary: Data class for EPS (power grid) damage inflicted by an avatar during boarding, capturing the boarding avatar, both ships, and three damage numbers.

nodes.name: AvatarBoardingEpsDamageResult
nodes.summary: Typed DTO with fields avatar, ship, target, directEnergyDamage, countermeasuresEnergyDamage, and hullDamage — describing the multi-stat outcome of an avatar boarding attack.

nodes.name: avatar-boarding-intercepted-result.js
nodes.summary: Data class for a boarding attempt that was intercepted, holding the boarding avatar and the target ship.

nodes.name: AvatarBoardingInterceptedResult
nodes.summary: Typed DTO with fields avatar (AvatarResult) and ship (ShipNameAndNccResult) describing an intercepted boarding.

nodes.name: avatar-boarding-retreat-result.js
nodes.summary: Data class for an avatar retreating from a boarding action, holding the avatar and the target ship.

nodes.name: AvatarBoardingRetreatResult
nodes.summary: Typed DTO with fields avatar (AvatarResult) and ship (ShipNameAndNccResult) describing a boarding retreat.

nodes.name: avatar-boarding-weapon-sabotage-result.js
nodes.summary: Data class for an avatar sabotaging weapons during boarding, holding the avatar and the target ship.

nodes.name: AvatarBoardingWeaponSabotageResult
nodes.summary: Typed DTO with fields avatar (AvatarResult) and ship (ShipNameAndNccResult) describing a weapon-sabotage boarding outcome.

nodes.name: avatar-damage-reduction-result.js
nodes.summary: Data class for an avatar successfully reducing incoming damage, referencing the avatar, an affected colony, and the ship.

nodes.name: AvatarDamageReductionResult
nodes.summary: Typed DTO with fields avatar (AvatarResult), colony (ColonyNameAndIdResult), and ship (ShipNameAndNccResult) describing a successful damage-reduction event.

nodes.name: avatar-damage-resuction-failure-result.js
nodes.summary: Data class for a failed avatar damage-reduction event, referencing the avatar, an affected colony, and the ship. The file name contains a typo ('resuction' instead of 'reduction') that must be preserved.

nodes.name: AvatarDamageReductionFailureResult
nodes.summary: Typed DTO with fields avatar, colony, and ship describing a damage-reduction attempt that failed.

nodes.name: avatar-decoy-drone-failure-result.js
nodes.summary: Data class for a failed avatar decoy-drone deployment, referencing the avatar, a colony, and the ship.

nodes.name: AvatarDecoyDroneFailureResult
nodes.summary: Typed DTO with fields avatar, colony, and ship describing a failed decoy-drone deployment.

nodes.name: avatar-decoy-drone-success-result.js
nodes.summary: Data class for a successful avatar decoy-drone deployment, referencing the avatar, a colony, and the ship.

nodes.name: AvatarDecoyDroneSuccessResult
nodes.summary: Typed DTO with fields avatar, colony, and ship describing a successful decoy-drone deployment.

nodes.name: avatar-emergency-shield-activation-failure-result.js
nodes.summary: Data class for a failed emergency shield activation by an avatar, holding the avatar and the ship.

nodes.name: AvatarEmergencyShieldActivationFailureResult
nodes.summary: Typed DTO with fields avatar and ship describing a failed emergency shield activation.

nodes.name: avatar-emergency-shield-activation-result.js
nodes.summary: Data class for a successful emergency shield activation by an avatar, holding the avatar and the ship.

nodes.name: AvatarEmergencyShieldActivationResult
nodes.summary: Typed DTO with fields avatar and ship describing a successful emergency shield activation.

nodes.name: avatar-energy-recovery-result.js
nodes.summary: Data class for an avatar recovering energy, holding the avatar and the ship.

nodes.name: AvatarEnergyRecoveryResult
nodes.summary: Typed DTO with fields avatar and ship describing avatar energy recovery.

nodes.name: avatar-out-of-drones-result.js
nodes.summary: Data class for an avatar running out of a specific drone type, referencing the avatar, colony, ship, and the drone type enum.

nodes.name: AvatarOutOfDronesResult
nodes.summary: Typed DTO combining avatar, colony, ship, and a drone-type enum value to describe an 'out of drones' condition.

nodes.name: avatar-pilot-maneuver-cooldown-result.js
nodes.summary: Data class for a pilot maneuver being on cooldown, holding the avatar and the ship.

nodes.name: AvatarPilotManeuverCooldownResult
nodes.summary: Typed DTO with fields avatar and ship describing a pilot maneuver in cooldown.

nodes.name: avatar-pilot-maneuver-failure-result.js
nodes.summary: Data class for a failed pilot maneuver, holding the avatar and the ship.

nodes.name: AvatarPilotManeuverFailureResult
nodes.summary: Typed DTO with fields avatar and ship describing a failed pilot maneuver.

nodes.name: avatar-pilot-maneuver-result.js
nodes.summary: Data class for a generic pilot maneuver result, holding the avatar and the ship.

nodes.name: AvatarPilotManeuverResult
nodes.summary: Typed DTO with fields avatar and ship describing a pilot maneuver (generic base type).

nodes.name: avatar-pilot-maneuver-success-result.js
nodes.summary: Data class for a successful pilot maneuver, holding the avatar and the ship.

nodes.name: AvatarPilotManeuverSuccessResult
nodes.summary: Typed DTO with fields avatar and ship describing a successful pilot maneuver.

nodes.name: avatar-weak-point-miss-result.js
nodes.summary: Data class for an avatar missing a weak-point attack, holding the avatar and the ship.

nodes.name: AvatarWeakPointMissResult
nodes.summary: Typed DTO with fields avatar and ship describing a weak-point attack that missed.

nodes.name: avatar-weapon-damage-increase-result.js
nodes.summary: Data class for an avatar increasing its weapon damage, referencing the avatar, a colony, and the ship.

nodes.name: AvatarWeaponDamageIncreaseResult
nodes.summary: Typed DTO with fields avatar, colony, and ship describing a weapon-damage increase event.

nodes.name: declare-war-result.js
nodes.summary: Data class for a war-declaration log line, holding the declaring player (nullable).

nodes.name: DeclareWarResult
nodes.summary: Tiny typed DTO with a single optional `player` field of type PlayerNameAndIdResult.

nodes.name: energy-damage-result.js
nodes.summary: Data class for an energy-damage log line, holding the affected ship and energy-damage statistics.

nodes.name: EnergyDamageResult
nodes.summary: Typed DTO with multiple energy-damage fields plus a ship reference, used when an avatar or weapon drains the ship's energy pool.

nodes.name: environment-cerulean-result.js
nodes.summary: Data class for a Cerulean-environment log event, holding a ship reference captured via the ship-name subroutine.

nodes.name: EnvironmentCeruleanResult
nodes.summary: Typed DTO with ship fields used when the game logs a Cerulean-environment effect.

nodes.name: hull-damage-result.js
nodes.summary: Data class for a hull-damage log line, holding the affected ship and hull-damage counters.

nodes.name: HullDamageResult
nodes.summary: Typed DTO with ship and damage fields used to record hull damage dealt to a ship.

nodes.name: item-weapon-overload-result.js
nodes.summary: Data class for an item-weapon-overload log line, holding the target ship and the damage-increase percentage.

nodes.name: ItemWeaponOverloadResult
nodes.summary: Typed DTO with ship (target) and damageIncrease (number) fields produced by the item-weapon-overload parser.

nodes.name: salvage-result.js
nodes.summary: Data class representing a parsed salvage event, capturing the debris field ship, number of resources extracted, and energy used.

nodes.name: shield-damage-result.js
nodes.summary: Data class for a shield damage event, capturing the affected ship, damage amount, remaining shield strength, and shield collapse flag.

nodes.name: system-blockade-caught-result.js
nodes.summary: Data class for a ship being caught in a system blockade, capturing the ship and the resulting flight range loss.

nodes.name: tractor-beam-directed-result.js
nodes.summary: Data class for a tractor beam being directed at a ship, capturing the target ship and its owning player character.

nodes.name: salvage-type.js
nodes.summary: Line type parser for German-language salvage events that extracts the debris field, resources extracted, and energy cost via the ShipNameAndNcc subroutine.

nodes.name: shield-damage-type.js
nodes.summary: Bilingual line type parser for shield damage events with German and English regex patterns, extracting the affected ship, damage amount, and shield collapse state.

nodes.name: system-blockade-caught-type.js
nodes.summary: Line type parser for German-language system blockade events, extracting the caught ship and the resulting flight range loss.

nodes.name: avatar-result.js
nodes.summary: Data class for parsed avatar results, capturing name, item ID, and job, with a human-readable display string formatter.

nodes.name: colony-name-and-id-result.js
nodes.summary: Data class for parsed colony name and ID, with a display string formatter combining both.

nodes.name: player-name-and-id-result.js
nodes.summary: Data class for parsed player character results, capturing name, ID, and optional ID prefix, with a display string formatter.

nodes.name: ship-name-and-ncc-result.js
nodes.summary: Data class for parsed ship results, capturing name, NCC, optional NCC prefix, and ship class, with a display string formatter.

nodes.name: avatar.js
nodes.summary: Regex subroutine that parses avatar mentions into a structured AvatarResult, mapping the localized job string to the AvatarJob enum in both English and German.

nodes.name: colony-name-and-id.js
nodes.summary: Regex subroutine that parses colony name and ID mentions from log text into a ColonyNameAndIdResult.

nodes.name: SalvageResult
nodes.summary: Data class for a parsed salvage event with the debris field ship, number of resources extracted, and energy used.

nodes.name: ShieldDamageResult
nodes.summary: Data class for a parsed shield damage event holding the target ship, damage amount, remaining shield strength, and a collapse flag.

nodes.name: SystemBlockadeCaughtResult
nodes.summary: Data class for a parsed system blockade catch, holding the caught ship and the flight range loss value.

nodes.name: TractorBeamDirectedResult
nodes.summary: Data class for a parsed tractor beam event, holding the target ship and the target's owning player character.

nodes.name: SalvageType
nodes.summary: Parses German salvage log lines by combining a ship name/NCC subroutine with numeric groups for resources extracted and energy used, and registers the debris field in statistics.

nodes.name: ShieldDamageType
nodes.summary: Parses shield damage log lines in both German and English, extracting the ship, damage amount, remaining shield strength, and collapse flag.

nodes.name: SystemBlockadeCaughtType
nodes.summary: Parses German system blockade catch log lines, extracting the caught ship and the flight range loss value.

nodes.name: AvatarResult
nodes.summary: Parsed avatar data holding name, item ID, and job; provides a human-readable display string.

nodes.name: ColonyNameAndIdResult
nodes.summary: Parsed colony data holding name and ID; provides a human-readable display string.

nodes.name: PlayerNameAndIdResult
nodes.summary: Parsed player character data holding name, ID, and optional ID prefix; provides a human-readable display string.

nodes.name: ShipNameAndNccResult
nodes.summary: Parsed ship data holding name, NCC, optional NCC prefix, and ship class; provides a human-readable display string.

nodes.name: Avatar
nodes.summary: Regex subroutine that matches an avatar mention and maps the localized job string to the AvatarJob enum for both English and German.

nodes.name: ColonyNameAndId
nodes.summary: Regex subroutine that matches a colony name and ID mention and returns a ColonyNameAndIdResult.

nodes.name: expression.js
nodes.summary: Base class for regex subroutines, providing pattern matching, subroutine definition, and a matchResult interface for use in parent regex patterns.

nodes.name: item.js
nodes.summary: Regex subroutine that parses item mentions with optional item ID and required type ID into an ItemResult.

nodes.name: ship-name-and-ncc.js
nodes.summary: Regex subroutine that parses ship mentions with optional NCC prefix, NCC, and ship class into a ShipNameAndNccResult.

nodes.name: avatar-statistics.js
nodes.summary: Aggregator for avatar statistics, registering avatar parse results and providing lookups by item ID while avoiding duplicate registration.

nodes.name: colony-statistics.js
nodes.summary: Aggregator for colony statistics, registering colony parse results and providing lookups by ID or name with idempotent registration.

nodes.name: individual-avatar-statistics.js
nodes.summary: Per-avatar counters for total/successful actions, damage reductions by type (hull/shield/energy), and avoided shots with weapon-name-based inference of base damage.

nodes.name: individual-colony-statistics.js
nodes.summary: Per-colony data holder for the colony's name and ID, with a basic-data check for registration completeness.

nodes.name: individual-player-character-statistics.js
nodes.summary: Per-player-character data holder for name, ID, ID prefix, and a deduplicated list of owned ships (linked two-way with IndividualShipStatistics).

nodes.name: individual-ship-statistics.js
nodes.summary: Comprehensive per-ship statistics tracking damage received/dealt by type, armor absorption/penetration, kills, destruction state, weapon shot history, and a merge utility for combining data across ship references.

nodes.name: player-character-statistics.js
nodes.summary: Aggregator for player character statistics, registering player parse results and providing lookups by ID or name with idempotent registration.

nodes.name: ship-statistics.js
nodes.summary: Aggregator for ship statistics, registering both full (NCC) and name-only ship parse results with lookups by NCC or name and support for late NCC updates.

nodes.name: statistics.js
nodes.summary: Top-level statistics coordinator that owns the per-entity aggregators (ships, players, colonies, avatars) and processes sequences of log lines representing a single attack to compute shot outcome, damage multipliers from avatar effects, and ship destruction.

nodes.name: weapon-shot.js
nodes.summary: Immutable data class capturing all information about a single weapon shot: raw and effective damage components, hit outcome, armor interactions, damage multiplier, and the firing/target ships.

nodes.name: Expression
nodes.summary: Base class for all regex subroutines, providing pattern registration, match execution, and a matchResult interface.

nodes.name: Item
nodes.summary: Regex subroutine that matches an item mention with optional item ID and required type ID, returning an ItemResult.

nodes.name: ShipNameAndNcc
nodes.summary: Regex subroutine that matches a ship name with optional NCC prefix, NCC, and ship class, returning a ShipNameAndNccResult.

nodes.name: AvatarStatistics
nodes.summary: Aggregator holding all avatar statistics with registration of AvatarResult into IndividualAvatarStatistics and lookup by item ID, ensuring uniqueness per item ID.

nodes.name: ColonyStatistics
nodes.summary: Aggregator holding all colony statistics with lookups by ID or name and registration that back-fills basic colony data on first encounter.

nodes.name: IndividualAvatarStatistics
nodes.summary: Per-avatar counters tracking total/successful actions, success rate, damage reductions by type (hull, shield, energy), and avoided shots with weapon-name-based inference of base damage.

nodes.name: IndividualColonyStatistics
nodes.summary: Per-colony data holder for the colony's name and ID with a basic-data check for registration completeness.

nodes.name: IndividualPlayerCharacterStatistics
nodes.summary: Per-player-character data holder for name, ID, ID prefix, and a deduplicated list of owned ships (linked two-way with IndividualShipStatistics via setOwner/_addShip).

nodes.name: IndividualShipStatistics
nodes.summary: Per-ship counters for damage received/dealt by type, armor absorption/penetration, kills, destruction state, weapon shot history, and a merge utility for combining ship entries across references.

nodes.name: PlayerCharacterStatistics
nodes.summary: Aggregator holding all player character statistics with lookups by ID or name and idempotent registration of PlayerNameAndIdResult.

nodes.name: ShipStatistics
nodes.summary: Aggregator holding all ship statistics with lookups by NCC or name, registration handling both full and name-only parse results, and support for late NCC updates.

nodes.name: Statistics
nodes.summary: Top-level statistics container composing per-entity aggregators and processing log-line sequences (attacks) to build weapon shots, apply avatar damage modifiers, and handle ship destruction events.

nodes.name: WeaponShot
nodes.summary: Immutable record of a single weapon shot's raw and effective damage components, hit outcome, armor interactions, damage multiplier, and the firing/target ships.

nodes.name: beam-direction.js
nodes.summary: Defines the BeamDirection enum with two states (toTarget/fromTarget) used to indicate the direction of resource and item beam transfers in combat logs.

nodes.name: BeamDirection
nodes.summary: Two-valued enum (toTarget, fromTarget) representing the direction of a beam event in log lines, built on the external Enumify library.

nodes.name: beam-resource.js
nodes.summary: Defines the BeamResource enum enumerating the categories of resources that can be transported via beaming (crew, energy, items, warp core, etc.).

nodes.name: BeamResource
nodes.summary: Enum extending Resource to represent resource categories that can be beamed between ships, including crew, energy, larne, items, and warp core.

nodes.name: building-type.js
nodes.summary: Defines the BuildingType enum listing every colony building recognized in the game (mines, factories, defensive structures, credit buildings, exotic and unobtainable types).

nodes.name: BuildingType
nodes.summary: Large enum cataloging approximately 70 building types grouped by category (resource production, military, science, credit, exotic), used to match German/English building names from log lines.

nodes.name: building-armor-absorption-type.js
nodes.summary: Parses the German log line emitted when a building's armor absorbs part of an incoming weapon hit, producing a result with surface coordinates, weapon name, absorption points, and remaining weapon strength.

nodes.name: BuildingArmorAbsorptionType
nodes.summary: Line-type subclass that defines a German regex matching armor-absorption messages and builds a BuildingArmorAbsorptionResult from the captured groups.

nodes.name: building-damage-type.js
nodes.summary: Parses the German log line emitted when a weapon damages a building, producing a result with weapon name, building, surface position, damage taken, and remaining hull strength.

nodes.name: BuildingDamageType
nodes.summary: Line-type subclass that defines a German regex matching building damage messages and builds a BuildingDamageResult from the captured groups, tagged as battle and weapon shot result.

nodes.name: fire-on-cloaked-building-type.js
nodes.summary: Parses the German log line emitted when a weapon fires on a cloaked building (no target details visible), producing a result with weapon name and strength.

nodes.name: FireOnCloakedBuildingType
nodes.summary: Line-type subclass that defines a German regex matching fire-on-cloaked-building messages and builds a FireOnCloakedBuildingResult, tagged as battle and weapon shot result.

nodes.name: fire-weapon-type.js

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: stne-log-parser is an ESM JavaScript library that turns raw STNE in-game log blobs into structured LogEntry / LogLine objects. The package manifest (package.json) declares the runtime stack -- dayjs for date handling, enumify for closed value types, the `regex` library for tagged-template pattern composition -- plus the webpack 5 / babel / jest toolchain that bundles the library as a UMD module consumable in both Node and the browser.
tour.languageLesson: Yarn's `nodeLinker: node-modules` setting (in .yarnrc.yml) forces a traditional node_modules tree instead of Plug'n'Play, so the project remains compatible with Jest's resolver and the webpack bundler without extra shims.

tour.title: Public API Entry
tour.description: src/index.js is the package's public surface: it re-exports StneLogParser, LogEntry, LogLine, the bundled line-type collections, and the `enum` / `statistics` / `regex` namespaces as named exports. The default-exported StneLogParser class (src/stne-log-parser.js) is the high-level entry point that consumers instantiate to parse log text -- everything else is plumbing that the index module re-exports for advanced users.
tour.languageLesson: Webpack `require.context` (or its ESM equivalent `import.meta.webpackContext`) lets a barrel file glob a whole directory and auto-collect every module's default export, so adding a new line-type file does not require touching any import list.

tour.title: Central Data Model
tour.description: LogEntry is the top-level result: it bundles a parsed LogMessage header, an array of LogLine objects, and optional Statistics. LogEntry exposes static `parseLogEntries` / `parseLogMessage` / `preprocessLogMessage` entry points plus an instance `findAttacks` that groups lines into attack sequences. LogLine represents a single parsed row -- it carries the source text, detection flag, the matched GenericType parser, the parse result, the language, and a `populateStatistics` hook that the statistics layer (Step 6) calls.

tour.title: Log Line Parsing Pipeline
tour.description: LogHeadParser (src/regex/log-head-parser.js) splits a raw multi-entry log blob into individual LogMessage headers by matching a tagged regex over German / English / Spanish direction keywords, the player-name subroutine, and the date format. Each header is then handed to LogLine.parse, which walks every line-type parser -- all of which extend GenericType (src/line-type/generic-type.js) -- until one reports a detect hit. The example parsers `armor-absorption-type` and `hull-damage-type` show the canonical shape: a static `_regexByLanguage` map, a static `detect` / `parse` pair, and a `populateStatistics` hook.
tour.languageLesson: GenericType uses a class with only static methods as a base. Subclasses override `_buildResultObject` and `populateStatistics`; the base's static `detect` and `parse` look up the per-language regex from `_regexByLanguage` and dispatch to the subclass hooks.

tour.title: Regex Subroutines
tour.description: Below the line-type parsers sits a layer of reusable named-capture regex patterns built with the `regex` library's tagged templates. The Expression base class (src/regex/subroutine/expression.js) provides pattern registration and a `matchResult` interface. Concrete subroutines -- ship-name-and-ncc (the most-referenced subroutine in the codebase, fan-in 58), player-name-and-id, and avatar (which maps the localized job string to the AvatarJob enum) -- compose into the per-language main patterns of each line type via the addSubroutines helper in src/util/regex-helper.js.
tour.languageLesson: The `regex` library's tagged-template literals (e.g. `` regex`^${shipName} hits ${target}$` ``) let the parser splice subroutine patterns into a parent pattern as named capture groups at compile time, avoiding the brittleness of building regexes by string concatenation.

tour.title: Statistics Aggregation
tour.description: src/statistics/statistics.js is the top-level coordinator: it owns the per-entity aggregators (ships, players, colonies, avatars) and processes sequences of log lines representing a single attack to compute shot outcomes, damage multipliers from avatar effects, and ship destruction. individual-ship-statistics.js tracks per-ship damage received / dealt by type, armor absorption / penetration, kills, destruction state, and weapon-shot history, with a `merge` utility that combines data across ship references. The two files form a tightly-coupled cluster with mutual imports.

tour.title: Enums and Foundation
tour.description: The enum layer (src/enum/) holds closed value types that constrain and label parsed data; line-tag.js in particular is the most-imported file in the codebase (fan-in 74) and is what gives each parsed line its battle / transport / ship-movement / avatar-action classification. Around the enums sit the foundation barrels -- src/line-type.index.js, src/regex.index.js, src/statistics.index.js, and their preprocessor / enum siblings -- which use `import.meta.webpackContext` to auto-discover every file in their directory and expose both a class list and a name-keyed dict. The preprocessor base (src/preprocessor/generic-preprocessor.js) is the abstract entry for log-message preprocessors that mutate the raw log string before parsing.

tour.title: Browser Demo and Webpack Build
tour.description: The library ships with an in-browser test page (public/index.html, with styling in public/style.css) that lets a user paste a raw log blob into a contenteditable input and see parsed lines, per-ship statistics, and a line-type support matrix rendered in real time. src/demonstration/index.js wires this up: it exposes the parser on `window.stneLogParser` and runs the three section setup routines (line-types, log-parser input, log-parser stats). Webpack builds both bundles from webpack.common.js: a library UMD entry (the published parser) and a demonstration entry (the test page), with webpack.dev.js adding inline source maps and webpack.prod.js stripping them for production. The src/util/webpack-import-helper.js module is the runtime side of the barrel pattern used everywhere else in the library.
tour.languageLesson: A multi-entry webpack config produces multiple bundles in one build. The `common` config declares the entries; the `dev` and `prod` configs each merge it with `webpack-merge` and add mode-specific options (source maps for dev, optimization and asset minimization for prod).

tour.title: CI and Dependency Automation
tour.description: The .github/workflows/node.js.yml pipeline runs `yarn install` followed by `yarn test` across a Node 20.x / 22.x / 24.x matrix on every push and pull request targeting main -- a tight loop that catches regressions in the line-type parsers and statistics layer as soon as a change lands. Dependabot (.github/dependabot.yml) supplements this by opening weekly pull requests for npm dependency updates, keeping dayjs, enumify, regex, webpack, and the rest of the toolchain current without manual maintenance.
tour.languageLesson: A GitHub Actions matrix strategy (e.g. `matrix: { node-version: [20.x, 22.x, 24.x] }`) expands one job declaration into N parallel jobs, one per entry. This catches version-specific issues -- for example, a regex feature that behaves differently between Node 20 and 24 -- without writing separate workflows.
